notes: Every entry's contact details were verified against the official source listed in `source` on `verified_date`. Contributions: see ARCHITECTURE.md — official sources only, verified_date = the date you actually checked.

resources.name: 911 — Emergency Services
resources.description: Police, fire, and ambulance for any life-threatening emergency happening right now.
resources.notes: If you can't speak safely, many areas accept texts to 911 — try text if calling is dangerous.

resources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
resources.description: Free, confidential support 24/7 for anyone in emotional distress or suicidal crisis — call, text, or chat.
resources.notes: Veterans and service members: press 1. Para español: presiona 2. ASL services available.

resources.name: Crisis Text Line
resources.description: Free 24/7 text-based support with a trained volunteer crisis counselor, for any kind of crisis.
resources.notes: Also on WhatsApp — see their site. Serves the US and Puerto Rico.

resources.name: Veterans Crisis Line
resources.description: Free, confidential crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families — no VA enrollment needed.

resources.name: Disaster Distress Helpline (SAMHSA)
resources.description: Crisis counseling for anyone experiencing emotional distress related to a natural or human-caused disaster.
resources.notes: Para español: presiona 2. Deaf/HoH: call from a videophone or use the ASL option on samhsa.gov.

resources.name: The Trevor Project
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis support for LGBTQ+ young people — phone, text, or chat.
resources.notes: Contact Trevor directly — the former 988 'press 3' LGBTQ+ youth option no longer operates.

resources.name: Trans Lifeline
resources.description: Peer-support hotline run by and for trans and questioning people. No non-consensual active rescue.
resources.notes: Hours vary — check translifeline.org/hotline for the current schedule before calling.

resources.name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 support, safety planning, and referrals to local shelters and services.
resources.notes: TTY: 1-800-787-3224. Deaf/DeafBlind/HoH videophone: 1-855-812-1001. Use a safe device — consider the quick-exit button.

resources.name: National Sexual Assault Hotline (RAINN)
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 support for survivors of sexual assault; routes to local providers.
resources.notes: Online chat is anonymous.

resources.name: National Human Trafficking Hotline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 help and safety planning for victims and survivors of human trafficking; anonymous tips accepted.
resources.notes: Deaf/HoH: dial 711 (relay).

resources.name: Childhelp National Child Abuse Hotline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 counseling for children, teens, and adults with concerns about child abuse or neglect.
resources.notes: A support line, not an emergency dispatcher — call 911 if a child is in immediate danger.

resources.name: National Runaway Safeline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 support for youth (roughly 12–21) who are thinking about running away, are homeless, or in crisis — and for concerned adults.

resources.name: Poison Control (Poison Help)
resources.description: Free, expert guidance from nurses and pharmacists for poison exposures — medicines, chemicals, bites, anything swallowed.
resources.notes: If someone is unconscious or not breathing, call 911 first.

resources.name: 211 — Local Help Line (United Way)
resources.description: One free call connects you to local food, housing, utility, and social services almost anywhere in the US.
resources.notes: Service is run by ~200 local 211 centers — offerings vary by county. Dial 211 or search 211.org.

resources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referral for substance use and mental health — connects you to local treatment and support groups.

resources.name: Feeding America — Find Your Local Food Bank
resources.description: Enter your ZIP code to find your local food bank and its network of 60,000+ food pantries and meal programs.
resources.hours: Website 24/7; each food bank sets its own hours
resources.notes: After finding your food bank, call them for the nearest pantry and hours.

resources.name: USDA National Hunger Hotline
resources.description: Talk to a real person about emergency food near you — pantries, meal sites, SNAP, and WIC.
resources.sms: Text FOOD (or SNAP, WIC) to 914-342-7744 for automated info
resources.notes: Español: 1-877-842-6273. Operated by Hunger Free America.

resources.name: SNAP (Food Stamps) — Apply in Your State
resources.description: Monthly grocery benefits for low-income households. Find your state's SNAP office, hotline, and application.
resources.notes: You apply in the state where you live — the directory lists every state's contacts.

resources.name: WIC — Nutrition Help for Moms & Young Children
resources.description: Food, nutrition support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women and children under 5.
resources.notes: If you're on Medicaid, SNAP, or TANF you likely qualify automatically on income.

resources.name: HUD Find Shelter
resources.description: Search shelters, food pantries, health clinics, and clothing assistance near you.
resources.hours: Website 24/7; providers set their own hours
resources.notes: Call a shelter before traveling to it — availability changes daily.

resources.name: findhelp.org
resources.description: Search free and reduced-cost programs by ZIP code — food, housing, money help, healthcare, legal aid, transport, and more.
resources.notes: The largest US social-care directory; programs maintain their own listings.

resources.name: LIHEAP — Help Paying Energy Bills (NEAR)
resources.description: Federal help with heating and cooling bills, shut-off emergencies, and weatherization. NEAR tells you where to apply locally.
resources.notes: Benefits come from your state/tribal agency — NEAR points you to the right application.

resources.name: Eldercare Locator
resources.description: Connects older adults and caregivers to local services — meals, transport, in-home care, housing, and benefits.
resources.notes: A public service of the US Administration for Community Living.

resources.name: Find Legal Aid (LSC)
resources.description: Free civil legal help for low-income people — housing, benefits, family, and consumer problems. Find your local legal aid office.
resources.notes: Generally for households at or below 125% of the federal poverty line. Self-help resources: lawhelp.org.

resources.name: Find a Health Center (HRSA)
resources.description: Community health centers provide medical and dental care regardless of insurance — you pay what you can afford on a sliding scale.
resources.notes: 16,000+ locations nationwide; many include pharmacies.

resources.name: FEMA / DisasterAssistance.gov
resources.description: Apply for federal disaster assistance (housing, repairs, other needs) after a federally declared disaster.
resources.notes: Only for federally declared disasters — the helpline can tell you if your area qualifies. Para español: presiona 2.

resources.name: HealthCare.gov Marketplace Call Center
resources.description: Free help comparing and enrolling in health insurance plans, including checking for subsidies and Medicaid eligibility.
resources.notes: TTY: 1-855-889-4325. Some states (e.g. CA, NY) run their own exchanges with separate call centers.

resources.name: 112 — European Emergency Number / Ευρωπαϊκός Αριθμός Έκτακτης Ανάγκης
resources.description: Police, ambulance, and fire across the EU — free from any phone, even without a SIM card. Στην Ελλάδα δέχεται και SMS.
resources.notes: Works EU-wide; Greek operators answer in Greek, English, and French.

resources.name: 100 — Ελληνική Αστυνομία (Police)
resources.description: Άμεση Δράση — Greek police emergency line.
resources.notes: For English or French, call 112.

resources.name: 166 — ΕΚΑΒ (Ambulance)
resources.description: Εθνικό Κέντρο Άμεσης Βοήθειας — Greek national ambulance service.
resources.notes: For English or French, call 112.

resources.name: 199 — Πυροσβεστική (Fire Service)
resources.description: Greek fire brigade emergency line — fires and rescue.
resources.notes: For English or French, call 112.

resources.name: 1018 — Γραμμή Παρέμβασης για την Αυτοκτονία (Suicide Intervention Line)
resources.description: Ανώνυμη, εμπιστευτική 24ωρη γραμμή για άτομα σε αυτοκτονική κρίση — στελεχωμένη από επαγγελματίες ψυχικής υγείας (Κλίμακα).
resources.notes: Αστική χρέωση από σταθερό ή κινητό. Email: help@suicide-help.gr

resources.name: 15900 — Γραμμή SOS για γυναίκες θύματα βίας (Domestic Violence Line)
resources.description: 24ωρη γραμμή για γυναίκες που υφίστανται βία — ψυχολογική στήριξη, νομικές πληροφορίες, παραπομπή σε συμβουλευτικά κέντρα και ξενώνες.
resources.notes: Email: sos15900@isotita.gr. Στελεχωμένη από ψυχολόγους και κοινωνικούς επιστήμονες.

resources.name: 197 — ΕΚΚΑ Γραμμή Άμεσης Κοινωνικής Βοήθειας (Social Solidarity Line)
resources.description: Δωρεάν 24ωρη γραμμή του Εθνικού Κέντρου Κοινωνικής Αλληλεγγύης — ψυχοκοινωνική στήριξη, πληροφόρηση και παραπομπές σε κοινωνικές υπηρεσίες.
resources.notes: Δωρεάν από σταθερό και κινητό.

resources.name: 1056 — Εθνική Γραμμή SOS «Το Χαμόγελο του Παιδιού»
resources.description: 24ωρη εθνική γραμμή για παιδιά και εφήβους — κακοποίηση, παραμέληση, εκφοβισμός — στελεχωμένη από ψυχολόγους και κοινωνικούς λειτουργούς.
resources.notes: Δωρεάν από σταθερό και κινητό. Επίσης: Ευρωπαϊκή Γραμμή 116111 από τον ίδιο φορέα.

resources.name: 10306 — Γραμμή Ψυχοκοινωνικής Υποστήριξης (Mental Health Support Line)
resources.description: Δωρεάν, ανώνυμη γραμμή ψυχολογικής υποστήριξης του Υπουργείου Υγείας — ψυχολόγοι όλο το 24ωρο.

resources.name: Find A Helpline
resources.description: Free directory of verified emotional-support helplines in 130+ countries — find one for your country in seconds.
resources.notes: Official directory partner of the International Association for Suicide Prevention.

resources.name: IASP Crisis Centres Directory
resources.description: The International Association for Suicide Prevention's directory of crisis centers across 100+ countries.

resources.name: Befrienders Worldwide
resources.description: Global network of ~350 emotional-support centers in 32 countries — find a listener by phone, email, or in person.
